title: Water drops frames in EoE
wikitext:
[]: Water drops frames in EoE             Quote Post by unz » Tue Oct 06, 2015 3:55 pm I lied I have no app to pick frames on the phone;0. I watched EoE last sunday and noticed the water drops go counterclockwise, never completing the cycle. I' ll pick the moments when I get to watch it again but I don't expect something revealing, put together they are like a montage thing developed from the tv show but the drops happen in different moments as milestones to Shinji's return to the surface and saving some of the day. So you have a drop going down, one dropping right in red water the moment humans turn into LCL (with a lateral shot of a couple of seconds as if you where watching things from a train), one dropping up along with Shinji. So yeah nothing groundbreaking, I wonder if I am missing anything? The very first drop happens at the beginning of the movie but it's the debris one, still it enstablishes a mirror theme and a lack of point of reference along with massive repetition. I'm going to read a bit too much into it but maybe parallels with music air movements (prelude, break, quickening, one more "final", yep rebuild's ones) can be made. I haven't watched death and rebirth but I heard mad people complaining about a scene of the children playing instruments in order of appearance in the tv show so that's all I make out of it, details.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Wed Oct 07, 2015 11:24 am Personally i never noticed it. Maybe you are onto something here. Next time i will see the film i will pay attention to the scenes you are referring.

[]:             Quote Post by unz » Wed Oct 07, 2015 11:38 am It's four drops: initial debris, one more going down, one going right and a final one going up. Will edit the moments here. If anything this probably ties with the music metaphor in the death n rebirth scene with instruments and the rebuild titles.

I watched EoE last sunday and noticed the water drops go counterclockwise, never completing the cycle. So you have a drop going down, one dropping right in red water the moment humans turn into LCL (with a lateral shot of a couple of seconds as if you where watching things from a train), one dropping up along with Shinji. So yeah nothing groundbreaking, I wonder if I am missing anything? The very first drop happens at the beginning of the movie but it's the debris one, still it enstablishes a mirror theme and a lack of point of reference along with massive repetition. I'm going to read a bit too much into it but maybe parallels with music air movements (prelude, break, quickening, one more "final", yep rebuild's ones) can be made. I haven't watched death and rebirth but I heard mad people complaining about a scene of the children playing instruments in order of appearance in the tv show so that's all I make out of it, details.
